hyperedges.label: Trip Execution Pipeline: API → Planner → Workers → Results
Search-Context Flow: Tool handlers → Cache-First Search → Elasticsearch → Result Update
Memory Persistence: Active Context → Compression → Storage

nodes.norm_label: pull active context from redis, compress via llm, persist to postgres,     and u

nodes.label: Dynamic Worker Model Pattern
nodes.rationale: Ephemeral workers spawned on demand, each handles one task, checks dependencies via Redis agent statuses, executes via tool registry, exits after completion. Prevents predefined agent brittleness and enables horizontal scaling.

nodes.label: Cache-First Search Pattern
nodes.rationale: Query Elasticsearch semantic index first (places/cafes/hotels sharded by region), fall back to Google Places API only on cache miss, re-index results. Reduces API costs and latency for repeated queries.

nodes.label: Autonomous Replanning Pattern
nodes.rationale: When estimated_cost exceeds budget_inr, system autonomously spawns hotel_planning task with budget_constraint='cheaper'. No human intervention required. Enables graceful adaptation to constraints discovered during execution.

nodes.label: Three-Layer Memory Architecture
nodes.rationale: Layer 1: Redis Hashes for active runtime context (6h TTL). Layer 2: PostgreSQL for compressed context and user preferences. Layer 3: Elasticsearch for semantic knowledge (places, hotels, cafes, transport). Prevents context explosion while maintaining semantic retrieval.

nodes.label: Task DAG with Explicit Dependencies
nodes.rationale: destination_research has no dependencies, transport/hotel/food depend on destination_research, itinerary_optimization depends on all three. Workers check depends_on list against Redis agent statuses before executing. Enables parallel execution within dependency boundaries.

links.source_location: NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL environment variables point to backend
Both handle real-time event streaming and state updates
Both cache and serve search results with freshness validation (7-day cache)
Trip context stored in Redis with TTL, mirrors database structure
Worker runner processes tasks from Redis Stream

hyperedges.label: Trip Execution Pipeline: API → Planner → Workers → Results
Search-Context Flow: Tool handlers → Cache-First Search → Elasticsearch → Result Update
Memory Persistence: Active Context → Compression → Storage
